title: COVID-19 has set a milestone for RNA vaccines: a tool superior to conventional vaccines
author: Hicham Bouabe
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Although mRNA vaccines have undergone extensive preclinical and clinical investigations in the last decade mostly in the field of therapeutic vaccination against cancer, and several clinical studies have been ongoing for RNA vaccines against some infectious diseases, it is coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) that accelerated and enabled the first approval of an RNA vaccine, and thus has set a milestone for a powerful, elegant, alternative tool to conventional vaccines, a tool with far-reaching prospective. 

 This opinion article discusses the advantages of COVID-19 mRNA vaccines over conventional vaccines, and suggests that RNA vaccines harbour the potential to build a simple, clean and fast universal vaccine formulation that can be easily adapted to evolving infectious diseases, enabling vaccine development in a time-range of weeks to few months. Moreover, required improvements for RNA vaccines are discussed, such as thermostability and prevention of virus transmission.

 This opinion article discusses the advantages of COVID-19 mRNA vaccines over conventional vaccines, and suggests that RNA vaccines harbour the potential to build a simple, clean and fast universal vaccine formulation that can be easily adapted to evolving infectious diseases, enabling vaccine development in a time-range of weeks to few months.
